titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Gustave Speth Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Camila Zorrilla Tessler

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 14, 2019 at 07:21

did.abstract: James Gustave Speth (1942-) is a lawyer, activist, author, and advocate for the environment. The James Gustave Speth Papers consist of Speth's professional papers from 1977 to 2000 and covers the bulk of his career in environmental law. In addition to his career in environmental law, Speth has also served as a fellow in research and innovation organizations such as the Tellus Institute.

langmaterial.language: The James Gustave Speth Papers are in English.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The James Gustave Speth Papers are open to researchers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The James Gustave Papers were a gift of James Gustave Speth in 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The James Gustave Speth Papers are arranged chronologically. It is made of up five series: Natural Resources Defense Council, Council on Environmental Quality, World Resources Institute, Clinton Transition, and United Nations Development Programme.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: James Gustave Speth (1942-) is a lawyer, activist, author, and advocate for the environment. He was born in Orangeburg, South Carolina, and spent his childhood there before attending Yale College (Jonathan Edwards, 1964). He then attended the University of Oxford as a Rhodes Scholar and finally Yale Law (JD, 1970). His early life in Orangeburg shaped his attitudes both towards both the environment and social policy. In 1965 he married his childhood sweetheart.

Upon finishing law school, Speth became one of the founding members and senior attorney of the Natural Resources Defense Council (NRDC). With the NRDC he brought civilian suits against the government on behalf of environmental issues. He left NRDC in 1977, to work for the Carter Administration as first a member, and then chair, of the White House Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ). During this time, Speth became increasingly aware of the dangers of climate change, and in 1979 he asked prominent ecologists to write a report on global warming.

Following his work on the CEQ he continued to advocate for the environment to launch and lead the World Resources Institute (WRI) in 1982 with the help of a MacArthur Grant. WRI continued to work on focusing global environmental attention on climate change and other environmental issues.

Shortly after the election of Bill Clinton in 1990, Speth joined the transition as the head of the environmental team. This position led him the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), where a recommendation from Vice-President Al Gore led him to become an administrator. During this time, he was deeply involved with the Resident Coordinator Program. The Resident Coordinator program stationed UNDP offices in underdeveloped regions around the world. He was also involved in United Nations Reform.

Speth left the UNDP in 1999 to return to Yale University. He became the dean of the School of Forestry and Environmental Science, where he spearheaded the building of Kroon Hall, a new building for the school. Kroon Hall is notable for its adherence to sustainability. As dean he taught classes that outlined his concerns on the environmental crisis, which resulted in the book Red Sky at Morning: America and the Crisis of the Global Environment. Following this, Speth wrote the book The Bridge at the Edge of the World, which came out of the DeVane Lecture series, also at Yale University.
Speth retired from Yale University in 2009 to accept a teaching position at Vermont, and during that time he wrote Angels by the River, his memoir.

In addition to his career in environmental law, Speth has also served as a fellow in research and innovation organizations such as the Tellus Institute. He also received awards for his work in law, development, and environmentalism.
Biographical information in this note is primarily drawn from James Gustave Speth, Angels by the River: A Memoir (White River Junction: Chelsea Green Publishing, 2014)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The James Gustave Speth Papers consist of Speth's professional papers from 1977 to 2000 and covers the bulk of his career in environmental law. This material also records his career in development of third world countries and the creation of international policies both on the behalf of the environment and the elimination of poverty. The collection is organized in five series: Natural Resources Defense Council, Council on Environmental Quality, World Resources Institute, Clinton Transition, and the United Nations Development Programme. These materials contain: correspondence, speeches, academic papers, administrative memorandums, press releases, publications, and meeting minutes. Also included are travel materials and photographs from events and professional travel. These photographs are mostly of individuals or professional groups and are mostly unidentified. This collection does not contain personal papers.
Material that related to his time as Dean of Yale University's School of Forestry and Environmental Science has been separated from the larger collection.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: 10 linear feet of material pertaining to Speth's time as the Dean of the Yale School of Forestry and Environmental Science has been separated and is now incorporated as a part of University Archives.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown.

subject.part: Conservation of natural resources -- Law and legislation -- Connecticut

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Natural Resources Defense Council (NRDC) series consists of Speth's professional work between the years 1968 and 1978.

The Natural Resources Defense Council is an organization founded by Yale Law graduates, including Speth, that focused on solving environmental issues through legal action. Speth's work the NRDC included bringing suits against the government regarding clean water that led to better implementation of Clean Water Act, as well as working extensively for the protection of wetlands.

This series documents the early years of Speth's professional life, as well as documenting some of the earliest work done by the NRDC. Materials include memos, legal cases, and some environmental reports with the Natural Resources Defense Council. Also included are articles, annual reports, appointments, and other documents between the years of 1980 and 2013 that document the continued work of the NRDC after Speth's time with the organization.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Natural Resources Defense Council series is organized chronologically.

did.unittitle: University of South Carolina Environmental Law Project
Scientists Institute for Public Information v. Atomic Energy Commission
Brief Reply Court of Appeals Scientists' Institute for Public Information v. Atomic Energy Commission

Natural Resources Defense Council Annual Report
A Force of Nature: Natural Resources Defense Council 25 Years on Earth
Natural Resources Defense Council Annual Report
Natural Resources Defense Council in Profile
Natural Resources Defense Council Oral History Interviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ) series documents Speth's time as a member, then later the head of the council during the Carter administration. The Council on Environmental Quality is a group that works out of the Executive Branch of the United States government that works on developing environmental policies and initiatives. During Speth's time, he worked closely both with the President and other governmental offices to address issues of energy, the environment, and conservation. Materials in this series include correspondence, memorandums, papers, and speeches written by Speth.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Council on Environmental Quality series is organized chronologically.

did.unittitle: Carter v. Nixon election pamphlets and articles
Nuclear policy and world hunger correspondence
Detailed fact sheet for new White House initiatives
Staff and steering committee memos and correspondence
Energy 1979: what happened and why, symbols are not the answer
Bills, reports, and draft comments and correspondence
Congress and presidential correspondence and memos
Environmentalist community notes, memos and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The World Resources Institute (WRI) series documents the inception of the World Resources Institute in 1982 and Speth's time in leadership. The WRI is a nonprofit organization dedicated to issues of global development, environmental protection, and promoting environmentally sound development for future generations. Speth founded the WRI in 1982 with the aid of a MacArthur Grant and led through 1992. Materials in this series consist of administrative documents, correspondence, papers, statements, and speeches, as well as Speth's daily planners and his schedule. Also included are documents through 2009 that detail the continued work of the WRI after Speth's time with the organization.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The World Resources Institute series is organized chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) series documents Speth's work in the UNDP between 1992 and 2000. The United Nations Development Programme is an organization that focuses on the eradication of poverty and assisting in the development of policies, institutions, and leadership in countries with high levels of poverty. The organization also focuses on sustainable development to reduce policies that lead to disasters and climate change. As the administrator of the UNDP, Speth oversaw several projects, including the Resident Coordinator Programme, as well as leading the UNDP through the United Nations Reform that happened in the mid-1990s. This series consists of seven groupings: Executive Committee, General files, General Assembly, Publications, Resident Coordinator, Speeches, Papers and Statements, United Nations Reform, and Photographs. Materials in this series include meeting minutes, administrative memos, papers and speeches written by Speth, articles, publications, internal reports, travel documentation, and travel photographs, primarily of events and resident coordinator officers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The United Nations Development Programme series is organized in seven groupings: Executive Committee, General files, General Assembly, Publications, Resident Coordinator, Speeches, Papers and Statements, United Nations Reform, and Photographs. These groupings are all arranged chronologically, except Publications, wihich is arranged alphabetically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As per donor request, material in boxes 77-79 are restricted until January 1 2020.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 82 and 83 are VHS tapes and may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.
